predictions.text: U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (FRED series DGS10) will print at or above 5.00% on at least one business day.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if the St. Louis Fed FRED daily observation for DGS10 is >= 5.00 for any date on or before 2026-06-30; otherwise NO.
evidence: Weak U.S. Treasury auction highlighted rising term-premium anxiety and war-risk sensitivity (MarketWatch, 2026-03-25): https://www.marketwatch.com/story/a-bad-treasury-auction-is-offering-a-glimpse-into-the-anxiety-on-wall-street-over-the-iran-war-fd17f7a6?mod=mw_rss_topstories
monitoring_signals: Daily DGS10 level on FRED
Treasury auction tails and bid-to-cover ratios
MOVE index / rates volatility and term premium commentary
Inflation surprise (CPI/PPI) and energy price shocks
predictions.rationale: Recent auction stress suggests higher term premium risk. A 5% print is plausible if inflation surprises persist or geopolitical risk reprices duration, but still not the base case over ~3 months.

predictions.text: At least one U.S. nominal coupon Treasury auction (2y/3y/5y/7y/10y/20y/30y) will have BOTH (a) a tail of >= 5.0 bps versus the When-Issued yield at the 1:00pm ET deadline AND (b) bid-to-cover <= 2.30.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if TreasuryDirect/auction results (or Treasury press release) for any listed tenor show high yield minus WI yield at 1:00pm ET >= 0.050% AND bid-to-cover ratio <= 2.30 for an auction settling on/before 2026-06-30; otherwise NO.
evidence: Weak Treasury auction highlighted term-premium anxiety (MarketWatch, 2026-03-25): https://www.marketwatch.com/story/a-bad-treasury-auction-is-offering-a-glimpse-into-the-anxiety-on-wall-street-over-the-iran-war-fd17f7a6?mod=mw_rss_topstories
monitoring_signals: Auction WI levels vs stop-out yields
Indirect bidder take-down and dealer share
Real-yield movements and inflation breakevens
Geopolitical escalation headlines impacting risk premia
predictions.rationale: Auction indigestion is already visible; the joint condition is stricter than a simple tail and should occur mainly in risk-off or supply-shock episodes.

predictions.text: EIA will report U.S. refinery utilization (operable utilization rate) at or above 95.0% for at least one week in the Weekly Petroleum Status Report.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if any EIA Weekly Petroleum Status Report published on/before 2026-06-30 shows 'Refinery utilization rate, operable' >= 95.0% for the reported week; otherwise NO.
evidence: TotalEnergies warned refining margins are at unprecedented highs, implying downstream bottlenecks (CNBC, 2026-03-24): https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/24/totalenergies-ceo-world-has-never-experienced-refining-margins-like-this-.html
monitoring_signals: Weekly EIA refinery utilization and crude runs
Unplanned refinery outages and maintenance deferrals
Crack spreads and gasoline/distillate inventory levels
Import/export flows of refined products
predictions.rationale: Exceptionally high refining margins tend to pull utilization toward operational limits unless outages intervene; a single 95%+ print is historically plausible into summer demand.

predictions.text: At least one major container carrier (Maersk, MSC, CMA CGM, Hapag-Lloyd, or ONE) will announce a new or increased security/war-risk surcharge for Middle East/Persian Gulf (incl. Hormuz-adjacent) container services of at least $500 per TEU (or $1,000 per FEU) effective within 30 days of the announcement.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if an official carrier notice/press release/tariff bulletin on/before 2026-05-31 specifies a new or increased surcharge meeting the threshold and explicitly tied to security/war risk for relevant Middle East/Persian Gulf routing; otherwise NO.
evidence: Iran signaled 'non-hostile' ships can transit Hormuz, implying selective disruption strategy (NYT, 2026-03-24): https://www.nytimes.com/2026/03/24/world/middleeast/iran-strait-of-hormuz-ships.html
Oil swung sharply amid de-escalation hopes and renewed conflict concerns (CNBC, 2026-03-25): https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/25/oil-price-wti-brent-gas-lng-trump-iran-talks-hormuz.html
monitoring_signals: Carrier customer advisories and tariff updates
War-risk insurance pricing and JWC listed areas
UKMTO advisories and incident reports
Freight indices and spot rate volatility
predictions.rationale: Even without full closure, selective interdiction risk often leads carriers to pass through higher insurance and operational costs via surcharges.

predictions.text: SK hynix will publicly file a U.S. SEC registration statement for a U.S.-listed ADR or other U.S. public listing (e.g., Form F-1, F-3, or equivalent) following its confidential submission.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if SEC EDGAR shows a publicly available registration statement filed by SK hynix (or depositary/issuer entity) for a U.S. listing/ADR on/before 2026-09-30; otherwise NO.
evidence: SK Hynix filed confidentially for a U.S. listing, spotlighting investor appetite for AI memory suppliers (CNBC, 2026-03-25): https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/25/sk-hynix-confidential-us-listing-adr-ai-memory.html
monitoring_signals: SEC EDGAR new filings by SK hynix / depositary banks
Underwriter announcements and exchange listing notices
Korea and U.S. media reporting on timing and valuation
Semiconductor cycle indicators and HBM demand guidance
predictions.rationale: A confidential filing often precedes a public filing within months if market windows remain open; AI-memory demand supports proceeding, though volatility could delay.

predictions.text: Arm will report (in an earnings release, call transcript, or annual report) that it has recognized revenue from selling or providing its own in-house CPU/processor product (not just IP licensing), explicitly distinguishing it from standard IP licensing/royalties.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if Arm public communications on/before 2026-12-31 explicitly state it has booked/recognized revenue from Arm-produced in-house CPU/processor products (or equivalent product sales/services) distinct from IP licensing/royalties; otherwise NO.
evidence: Arm launched its first in-house CPU with Meta as a debut customer (CNBC, 2026-03-24): https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/24/arm-launches-its-own-cpu-with-meta-as-first-customer.html
monitoring_signals: Arm quarterly results and segment commentary
Meta procurement/roadmap disclosures
Foundry/packaging partner announcements
Analyst questions about productization and revenue mix
predictions.rationale: If Arm truly shifts into selling CPU products, revenue recognition should follow initial deployments; timing and classification could slip depending on delivery model and accounting.

predictions.text: OpenAI will publicly launch a paid, generally available text-to-video generation offering for developers (API or equivalent) with published pricing and documentation.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if OpenAI publishes official docs/blog/release notes on/before 2026-12-31 describing a generally available developer-facing video generation model with explicit pricing (per second/minute/output or equivalent); otherwise NO.
evidence: OpenAI is shutting down Sora, showing rapid product churn and safety/operational constraints (X/Twitter post, 2026-03-24): https://twitter.com/soraofficialapp/status/2036532795984715896
monitoring_signals: OpenAI platform docs changes (models/pricing pages)
Developer newsletter / release notes
Compute capacity announcements and new safety policies
Competitive video model launches by peers
predictions.rationale: The shutdown suggests a reset, not abandonment; monetizable video generation is strategically valuable, but safety/compute constraints make timelines uncertain.

predictions.text: Meta will disclose a litigation settlement accrual or charge of at least $500 million specifically tied to child-safety/child sexual abuse material (CSAM) related lawsuits or investigations (including the New Mexico case), in a Form 10-Q/10-K or 8-K.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if Meta SEC filings on/before 2026-12-31 show a >=$500M litigation-related accrual/charge and the filing text links it to child-safety/CSAM-related matters; otherwise NO.
evidence: Jury ordered Meta to pay $375M for child-safety violations, a landmark courtroom loss (CNBC, 2026-03-24): https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/24/jury-reaches-verdict-in-meta-child-safety-trial-in-new-mexico.html
monitoring_signals: Meta 10-Q/10-K litigation footnotes and contingency disclosures
Appeal filings and settlement talks reported by major outlets
Other state AG actions and verdicts in related cases
Meta policy changes/engineering spend disclosures
predictions.rationale: A large verdict increases pressure for broader settlements/reserves, but appeals and aggregation across cases affect whether a single disclosed charge reaches the threshold.

predictions.text: A major rating agency (Moody’s, S&P, or Fitch) will downgrade at least one U.S.-focused private-credit fund/vehicle managed by Apollo, Blackstone, KKR, Ares, or Blue Owl by at least 2 notches OR to below investment grade (whichever is easier), citing credit deterioration or rising non-performing loans.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if a published rating action by Moody’s/S&P/Fitch on/before 2026-09-30 meets the downgrade condition and names the manager/fund/vehicle; otherwise NO.
evidence: Moody’s cut a KKR-linked private credit fund to junk amid rising bad loans (CNBC, 2026-03-24): https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/24/moodys-private-credit-fund-kkr-future-standard-junk.html
monitoring_signals: Rating agency surveillance reports and outlook changes
Private credit delinquency/non-accrual disclosures
Refinancing volumes and default rates in leveraged loans/high yield
Redemption pressure and NAV write-downs in semi-liquid vehicles
predictions.rationale: With stress already visible in at least one vehicle, additional downgrades are plausible if refinancing conditions stay tight or defaults rise.

predictions.text: Ukraine’s Air Force (or Reuters/NYT citing official Ukrainian statements) will report a single 24-hour period in which Russia launched at least 700 drones (UAVs) at Ukraine.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if an official Ukrainian Air Force statement (or Reuters/NYT report quoting it) on/before 2026-09-30 states >=700 drones launched in a 24-hour period; otherwise NO.
evidence: Russia launched a daytime strike with 500+ drones, underscoring accelerating drone-war scale (NYT, 2026-03-24): https://www.nytimes.com/2026/03/24/world/europe/russia-attack-drones-ukraine-lviv.html
monitoring_signals: Daily Ukrainian Air Force attack summaries
Russian production capacity indicators (Shahed/Geran and equivalents)
Air defense interception rate trends and ammunition constraints
Major escalation events (new offensives, retaliation cycles)
predictions.rationale: Attack sizes are trending upward; 700 is a meaningful further step and may occur if mass-production and saturation tactics continue, but it’s not yet routine.
